segments.paradiso-20-g24: "Fecesi voce quivi," uses the archaic middle/passive form fecesi (from fare + si) to mean "a voice was made/arose" — an impersonal, poetic way to say that a voice came into being. Quivi is an old adverb meaning "there"; the whole short clause functions as a detached narrative statement ("there a voice arose").
segments.paradiso-20-g25: In "e quindi uscissi per lo suo becco in forma di parole," e = "and." Quindi here means "then/thereupon." Uscissi is a poetic past form meaning "issued/came forth" (an intransitive past action); per + noun indicates the route or means ("through his beak"). "In forma di parole" is a prepositional phrase describing the manner: "in the form of words/speech."
segments.paradiso-20-g26: "quali aspettava il core ov’ io le scrissi." Quali is a relative/demonstrative "which/those (words)." Aspettava (imperfect) = "had been awaiting/was waiting for." "Il core" (the heart) is the subject of aspettava. Ov' (= ove) = "where/in which." Io scrissi is first-person past "I wrote"; le is the feminine plural pronoun "them" (the words) — so: "the words which the heart had been expecting where I wrote them."
segments.paradiso-20-g27: "La parte in me" = "the part of me" (subject). The relative clause "che vede e pate il sole" uses vede (present) "sees" and pate (archaic form of pare/patere) "seems/appears" — here "sees and seems to feel/experience the sun." "Ne l'aguglie mortali" = "in the mortal aguglie" (aguglie here = eagles); ne + l' = nelle in older spelling.
segments.paradiso-20-g28: Incominciommi is a poetic/archaic conjugation combining the verb "incominciar" with the clitic -mi; here it functions as an introductory verb meaning "they began (to speak) to me" ("cominciar mi" → "they began to me"). The form is archaic and enclitic; read as the start of direct speech addressed to Dante.
segments.paradiso-20-g29: "or" = "now," fisamente = "fixedly/steadily," riguardar = infinitive "to look at/regard." The reflexive/impersonal si + vole (third-person singular "wishes/desires") gives the sense "now wishes to look intently" (literally "now it/one wishes to regard fixedly").
segments.paradiso-20-g30: "perché d’i fuochi ond’ io figura fommi," gives the reason: perché = "because." D'i = di + i = "of the" (or "from the") fuochi = "fires." Ond' = "from which/whence." Io figura fommi is a compressed, poetic expression meaning "I was formed/made in figure/image" — fommi here indicates the result of those fires that gave Dante his figurative shape. The clause explains the origin or cause of the desire just expressed.
segments.paradiso-20-g31: "quelli onde l’occhio in testa mi scintilla," — quelli = "those (fires)." Onde = "from which." "L'occhio in testa" = "the eye in (my) head;" mi is the dative/ethical clitic ("to me"/"in me"). Scintilla is present indicative 3rd sing. "sparks/sparkles" (literally and figuratively: the eye's light). So: "those from which the eye in my head sparkles."
segments.paradiso-20-g32: "e’ di tutti lor gradi son li sommi." The clause states the conclusion: e = "and." "di tutti lor gradi" = "of all their degrees/ranks." "Son li sommi" = "they are the summits/the highest." Word order is inverted for emphasis: the predicate "son" + nominal phrase "li sommi" names what the previously mentioned things amount to.
segments.paradiso-20-g33: Colui is the demonstrative noun 'he who' introducing a person; che begins a relative clause. Luce is the 3rd-person singular present of lucere ('shines'); in mezzo per pupilla is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in the middle, by/through the pupil' with per + pupilla indicating instrument/means ('through the pupil').
segments.paradiso-20-g34: Fu is the 3rd-person singular past (passato remoto) of essere, 'was.' Il cantor is the predicate nominative 'the singer/poet.' De lo Spirito Santo is an archaic two-word form of 'del' + title and means 'of the Holy Spirit.'
segments.paradiso-20-g35: Che introduces a relative clause modifying 'cantor' ('who'). L’arca is the direct object ('the ark'); traslatò is a past form meaning 'moved/transferred' (3rd sing. passato); di villa in villa is an idiomatic repetition meaning 'from village to village' (di = from, in = into/to each place).
segments.paradiso-20-g36: Ora is temporal 'now'; conosce is 3rd-person singular present of conoscere, 'he knows/realizes.' Il merto (an archaic form of 'merito') means 'the merit/desert' and del suo canto specifies 'of his song.'
segments.paradiso-20-g37: In quanto here functions as 'insofar as' or 'inasmuch as.' Effetto 'effect/result' plus fu (past 'was') yields 'inasmuch as it was the result of his counsel' (del suo consiglio). The clause links the merit of the song to its being the outcome of his counsel.
segments.paradiso-20-g38: Per lo remunerar means 'because of / for the reward/recompense' (remunerar used as noun). Ch’è is the contraction che è 'which is,' and altrettanto here means 'the same' or 'equivalent,' completing the idea that the recompense is commensurate.
segments.paradiso-20-g39: Dei cinque is a partitive/identifying phrase 'of the five.' Che introduces the relative clause 'who make a circle around me' — mi is the indirect object 'around me' and fan (from fare) is 3rd-person plural present 'make/do.' Per ciglio means 'along/by the eyelid,' ciglio here meaning 'eyelash/edge.'
segments.paradiso-20-g40: Colui 'the one' begins the noun phrase; che is the relative 'who.' Più al becco means 'nearest to the beak' (più as comparative 'more/nearest,' al = a + il 'to the'). Mi s’accosta is 'approaches me': s’ is the clitic reflexive pronoun (si) attached to the verb, and accosta is 3rd-singular present 'approaches.'
segments.paradiso-20-g41: La vedovella is the direct object 'the little widow' (diminutive); consolò is 3rd-person singular past 'comforted.' Del figlio literally 'about/of the son' indicates the subject matter of the consolation.
segments.paradiso-20-g42: Ora conosce 'now he knows/realizes.' Quanto caro costa non seguir Cristo is a clause meaning 'how dearly it costs not to follow Christ': quanto = 'how/ how much,' caro = 'costly/dear,' costa = 3rd-singular present 'it costs,' and non seguir Cristo is an infinitive clause functioning as the thing that 'costs.'
segments.paradiso-20-g43: Per l’esperïenza di questa dolce vita e de l’opposta means 'by/through the experience of this sweet life and of the opposite (one).' Per marks the means; esperïenza is 'experience'; de l’opposta 'of the opposite' contrasts the two kinds of life.
segments.paradiso-20-g74: Poi appresso is a two-word adverbial phrase meaning 'then' or 'afterward.' 'Poi' is the standard adverb 'then/afterward'; 'appresso' here reinforces the temporal sense ('soon after'/'afterward').
segments.paradiso-20-g75: Con + definite article + occhio is an instrumental prepositional phrase 'with the eye' (here idiomatically 'with his gaze'); 'più acceso' is a comparative ('more kindled'/'more intense') where 'acceso' is a past participle used adjectivally to mean 'inflamed/ardent' or 'brightly lit.' Word order is normal: preposition + article + noun + modifier.
segments.paradiso-20-g76: This is the main reporting clause: 'lo benedetto segno mi rispuose' uses the past historic 'rispuose' (reply/replied) with indirect object clitic 'mi' = 'to me.' 'Lo benedetto segno' = 'the blessed sign' (lo + adjective + noun). The following purpose clause 'per non tenermi in ammirar sospeso' uses 'per' + infinitive to express purpose ('so as not to...') and 'tenermi' is the infinitive with clitic 'mi' attached, 'in ammirar sospeso' = 'suspended in admiration' (ammirar = infinitive used as noun-like; sospeso = past participle used adjectivally).
segments.paradiso-20-g77: Direct speech begins. 'Io veggio' is first-person present; in Dante 'veggio' functions as 'I see' (an archaic/poetic form). 'che tu credi queste cose' is a subordinate clause: 'that you believe these things.' 'perch’ io le dico' = 'because I say them' (perch' = perchè/because; 'le' = clitic 'them' referring to 'cose').
segments.paradiso-20-g78: 'Ma non vedi come' is a short adversative clause: 'but you do not see how.' 'Vedi' is present indicative 'you see' (or 'you perceive/understand'); 'come' here means 'how.' Word order is standard negative: 'ma' (but) + 'non' (not) + verb + complement.
segments.paradiso-20-g79: Sì che is a compound connective meaning 'so that' or 'therefore,' introducing the consequence clause that follows. 'Sì' here has the resultive sense rather than simply 'yes.'
segments.paradiso-20-g80: This conditional clause 'se son credute' uses 'se' = 'if' and 'son' (poetic contraction of 'sono') present indicative 'they are'; 'credute' is the past participle used adjectivally/passively 'believed' (i.e. 'if they are believed').
segments.paradiso-20-g81: 'sono ascose' is a present indicative passive/state: 'they are hidden' or 'they remain hidden.' 'Sono' = 'are' and 'ascose' = past participle 'hidden/secret,' here predicative.
segments.paradiso-20-g82: Fai is an imperative 'do' (addressing the reader/listener). Come 'as/like' introduces the comparison. Quei che la cosa per nome apprende ben describes 'those who learn the thing by name well' — apprende is 3rd-person singular present 'learns/grasps' and per nome ('by name') indicates learning only the name rather than the essence; ben modifies apprende ('well').
segments.paradiso-20-g83: Ma 'but' introduces the contrast. La sua quiditate intends 'its quidditas/essence' (quiditate is a learned/Latin-derived noun meaning 'essence'); veder non può is an inversion for 'cannot see/understand' (infinitive veder + negated modal può). Se is the conditional 'if/unless' (introducing the protasis), altri 'someone else,' and prome is a verb meaning 'brings it forward / shows it' in this context; if no one else presents it, the essence cannot be seen. (Note: several forms here are archaisms and learned borrowings.)
segments.paradiso-20-g84: This is a nominative phrase with an archaic verb: Regnum celorum = "the kingdom of heaven" (Regnum + genitive celorum). vïolenza pate is an older expression meaning "suffers/undergoes violence" (pate = patire, to suffer). da + X indicates the cause or source (here 'from ardent love and living hope'). The adjectives caldo and viva qualify the nouns that follow.
segments.paradiso-20-g85: che is a relative pronoun referring back to "caldo amore e ... viva speranza." vince is present indicative 3rd singular "overcomes/wins." la divina volontate is the direct object (the divine will); volontate is an archaic spelling of volontà.
segments.paradiso-20-g86: non a guisa che = "not in the manner that / not as"; guisa = "manner, way." l’omo a l’om (one man to another) is a comparative construction: it says this overcoming is not like one man’s dominance over another. Sobranza here means superiority/overplus in the sense of domination (archaic use).
segments.paradiso-20-g87: ma introduces the contrast: vince is again 3rd singular present "it overcomes/conquers." lei is a feminine pronoun (the divine will) as the thing being overcome. perché introduces the cause: vuole esser vinta = "wishes to be conquered," where voler + infinitive expresses desire and esser vinta is a passive infinitive "to be conquered."
segments.paradiso-20-g88: Simple coordinating conjunction linking clauses: e = "and."
segments.paradiso-20-g89: vinta is the past participle (feminine) used here in an absolute/parenthetical way: "and, conquered," — it qualifies the following action as taking place after or because of that conquest.
segments.paradiso-20-g90: vince is again 3rd singular present "conquers/wins." con + sua beninanza = "by/with her own benignity/kindness" (beninanza = benevolence, archaic). sua agrees as a possessive referring back to the feminine noun (the will); the clause says that once conquered, she conquers by her goodness.
segments.paradiso-20-g91: La prima vita del ciglio e la quinta is a compound subject: "the first life/motion of the eyelid and the fifth." vita del ciglio = "movement/moment of the eyelid/eye" (genitive). ti is the indirect object "you," fa + infinitive (fa maravigliar) is a causative/impersonal construction meaning "causes you to marvel/astonishes you."
segments.paradiso-20-g92: perché introduces the reason: ne is a locution meaning "in them / therein" (referring back to the two movements just named). vedi is 2nd singular present "you see." la regïon de li angeli dipinta is an object phrase with dipinta (past participle) used predicatively/participial: "the region of the angels painted/portrayed (there)."
segments.paradiso-20-g61: Ora conosce assai = 'now (it) knows much': Ora is an adverb meaning 'now'; conosce is 3rd-person singular present of conoscere ('knows'); assai is an adverb 'much'. Di quel che ’l mondo veder non può de la divina grazia is a noun clause object introduced by di quel che ('of that which'); '’l mondo veder non può' is a reduced infinitival clause (veder non può = 'cannot see'); de la divina grazia is a prepositional phrase 'of/from the divine grace' qualifying what the world cannot see.
segments.paradiso-20-g62: ben che sua vista non discerna il fondo = 'although its sight does not discern the depth'. Ben che is a fixed concessive conjunction meaning 'although'; sua vista ('its sight') is a noun phrase with a possessive adjective; discerna is present indicative of discernere 'discerns' and il fondo ('the depth') is the direct object. The clause concedes limited vision despite prior knowledge.
segments.paradiso-20-g63: Quale allodetta che ’n aere si spazia prima cantando = a simile introduced by Quale ('as'/'such as') followed by the simile noun allodetta (an archaic word for a small bird, 'lark'). ’n is the contracted form of in ('in') before a vowel; aere is 'air/sky'; si spazia is a pronominal verb meaning 'ranges/roams' (3rd-singular), and prima cantando is a temporal participial phrase 'first singing'.
segments.paradiso-20-g64: e poi tace contenta de l’ultima dolcezza che la sazia = 'and then falls silent, content with the ultimate sweetness that satiates it'. e poi ('and then') links the actions; tace is 3rd-singular present 'is silent'; contenta is a predicate adjective 'content/pleased'; de l’ultima dolcezza is a prepositional phrase 'of the last sweetness' and che la sazia is a relative clause 'which satisfies it'.
segments.paradiso-20-g65: tal mi sembiò l’imago de la ’mprenta de l’etterno piacere = 'such seemed to me the image of the imprint of eternal pleasure'. tal ('such') resumes the simile; mi is dative 'to me'; sembiò is passato remoto (3rd-singular) of sembiare 'seemed'; l’imago 'the image' and de la ’mprenta 'of the imprint' form the subject phrase; de l’etterno piacere 'of eternal pleasure' specifies whose imprint it is.
segments.paradiso-20-g66: al cui disio ciascuna cosa qual ell’ è diventa = 'at whose desire each thing becomes what it is'. Al cui is a relative prepositional phrase 'to/at whose' governing disio ('desire'); ciascuna cosa 'each thing' is the subject of diventa (3rd-singular present 'becomes'); qual ell’ è ('as it is' / 'what it is') is a predicative relative phrase giving the determination effected by that desire.
segments.paradiso-20-g67: E avvegna ch’io fossi al dubbiar mio lì quasi vetro a lo color ch’el veste = 'And although I was in my doubt there, almost like glass to the colour that clothes it'. Avvegna (here used concessively) corresponds to 'although'; ch’io fossi is a subordinate clause with fossi (archaic or subjunctive form) meaning 'I was' in the state of doubting; al dubbiar mio is a locative/possessive phrase 'in my doubt'; lì quasi vetro a lo color ch’el veste is a simile comparing the speaker to glass reflecting the color that clothes it.
segments.paradiso-20-g68: tempo aspettar tacendo non patio = 'I did not endure waiting silently for a time'. Tempo + infinitive (aspettar) expresses a span of time or the idea 'to wait a while'; tacendo ('being silent') is a gerund modifying aspettar; non patio is the negative main verb clause 'I did not suffer/ endure'—patio is an archaic/dialectal verb form meaning 'I endured' (here negated).
segments.paradiso-20-g69: ma de la bocca, = 'but from the mouth'. ma = 'but'; de la bocca ('from the mouth') indicates origin of the utterance—what follows issues from the speaker's mouth.
segments.paradiso-20-g70: «Che cose son queste?» = direct question 'What are these things?'. Che cose = 'what things' (an exclamatory/interrogative phrase); son is the contracted poetic form of sono ('are'); queste = 'these'. Word order is standard for a direct question in Dante's style.
segments.paradiso-20-g71: This segment is a punctuation mark (comma) separating clauses; no words to analyze.
segments.paradiso-20-g72: mi pinse con la forza del suo peso: = 'it pressed/pushed me with the force of its weight'. Mi is the direct object 'me'; pinse is passato remoto (3rd-singular) of an older/polite verb meaning 'pushed/pressed' (here rendered 'pressed/pushed'); con la forza del suo peso is an ablative/instrumental phrase expressing the means ('by the force of its weight').
segments.paradiso-20-g73: per ch’io di coruscar vidi gran feste = 'whereby/so that I, from the flashing, saw great feasts'. Per ch’io introduces a result clause ('so that I'); di coruscar ('from the flashing') is an ablative/causal phrase describing the visual effect; vidi is passato remoto 1st-singular 'I saw'; gran feste is the plural direct object 'great feasts' or 'great displays'.
segments.paradiso-20-g93: D’i is the contracted form of di + i, here meaning "from the." 'Corpi suoi' = 'their bodies' (possessive follows the noun). 'Non uscir' is a poetic/archaic verbal form meaning 'did not come out' (negative + verb); the verb is placed after the noun phrase for metrical inversion.
segments.paradiso-20-g94: 'Come' is the comparative/linking particle 'as' introducing the clause 'as you think.' 'Credi' is 2nd-person singular present indicative ('you believe/think') and directly addresses the addressee.
segments.paradiso-20-g95: A single-word vocative: 'Gentili' addresses the addressee (plural). In this context it functions as 'O Gentiles' (pagans) rather than the adjective 'kind.'
segments.paradiso-20-g96: 'Ma' is the coordinating conjunction 'but' introducing the contrast; 'Cristiani' is a plural noun in apposition to the vocative, correcting the addressee's assumption ('but Christians').
segments.paradiso-20-g97: ‘In ferma fede’ is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'in firm/steadfast faith' that qualifies what follows. The two repeated 'quel' are demonstrative pronouns ('that one' / 'the one') governing the genitive phrases 'd’i passuri' and 'd’i passi piedi' (literally 'of the passuri' and 'of the passi piedi'); the structure links those two persons/things as being steadfast in faith.
segments.paradiso-20-g98: 'Ché' = 'for/because' introducing an explanation. 'L’una' = 'the one (one of them),' and 'de lo ’nferno' is an archaic/genitive-style locative 'from the Inferno' (de + lo; the apostrophe marks the dropped initial vowel).
segments.paradiso-20-g99: ‘U’ (short for 'ove' or 'ur') = 'where.' 'Non si riede' uses the clitic 'si' impersonally/reflexively ('one does not return') with the verb 'riedere' (to return). 'Già mai' is an emphatic negation ('ever/at all' in negative contexts), and 'a buon voler' means 'of one's good will' = 'willingly/of one's own free will.' Word order is typical Dantean inversion for rhythm.
segments.paradiso-20-g100: 'Tornò' is passato remoto (3rd sing.) of 'tornare' = 'returned.' 'A l’ossa' = 'to the bones' (a + l' = al), i.e. back to the corpse/bones; semi-colon ends the clause.
segments.paradiso-20-g101: 'E ciò' = 'and this'; 'di viva spene' = 'of living hope' (di + noun), and 'fu mercede' is 'was a reward' (fu = past of essere, mercede = 'reward/wage/mercy'). The whole clause states that the return was the recompense of a living hope.
segments.paradiso-20-g102: This repeats 'di viva spene' as an emphatic apposition, reinforcing that the motive/reward was a living hope.
segments.paradiso-20-g103: 'Che' introduces a relative clause explaining the 'living hope.' 'Mise' is passato remoto (3rd sing.) of 'mettere' meaning 'put' or 'placed' (here: 'put power into'). 'La possa' is a nominalized 'possa' = 'the power/ability' (la + noun). 'Ne’ prieghi fatti a Dio' = 'into the prayers made to God' (ne' = in + article; 'fatti' past participle 'made'). 'Per suscitarla' = 'to raise/restore her' (gerund/infinitive purpose).
segments.paradiso-20-g104: 'Sì che' = 'so that/with the result that.' 'Potesse' is imperfect subjunctive of 'potere' ('might be able/could'), expressing potential/result. 'Sua voglia esser mossa' = 'her will might be moved' (infinitive construction: 'essere' + past participle 'mossa' = 'be moved'); the clause gives the intended effect of the prayers.
segments.paradiso-20-g105: L’anima glorïosa is the noun phrase "the glorious soul." onde si parla is a relative/adverbial phrase "of whom/which one speaks" (onde = 'of/from which'); si parla is an impersonal or passive construction meaning "is spoken of."
segments.paradiso-20-g106: tornata is a feminine past participle agreeing with anima: "having returned." ne la carne uses archaic ne = "into/in the" (here indicating re-entry into the body), so the participial phrase reads "having returned into the flesh."
segments.paradiso-20-g107: in che = "in which" (relative). fu poco = "she was little" — fu is the passato remoto (simple past) of essere, and poco means "for a short time" (elliptically: 'she remained there only a little while').
segments.paradiso-20-g108: credette is passato remoto 3rd singular "she believed/put her faith in." in lui = "in him." che potëa aiutarla is a relative / content clause "who could help her," where potëa is an archaic imperfect (potea = could) and aiutarla is the infinitive + object pronoun "to help her."
segments.paradiso-20-g109: e credendo is a causal gerund "and, believing" (showing circumstance). s’accese is reflexive perfect "she kindled herself / was set on fire" (si accese = became inflamed). in tanto foco di vero amor is a prepositional phrase "into so great a fire of true love."
segments.paradiso-20-g110: ch’ = che introducing a result: "that." a la morte seconda = "at/to the second death" (seconda qualifies morte). fu degna = passato remoto of essere + adjective "she was worthy." di venire a questo gioco = "to come/enter into this game/contest" where venire is the infinitive governed by degna di.
segments.paradiso-20-g111: L’ altra is the feminine singular noun phrase 'the other (one)'. L’ is the elided definite article (il → l’) placed before a vowel; altra is the adjective/noun 'other' agreeing in gender with the understood feminine subject.
segments.paradiso-20-g112: Per grazia is an ablative/instrumental phrase meaning 'by/through grace'; che begins a relative clause modifying 'grazia' (’a grace that…’). Da sì profonda fontana stilla uses da + noun to indicate source ('from so deep a fountain'); sì is the emphatic 'so', profonda modifies fontana, and stilla ('drop') is a poetic nominative describing what issues from the fountain.
segments.paradiso-20-g113: This is the continuating relative clause introduced by che: 'that no creature ever…'. Non + mai here creates the negative 'never' pattern (non … mai). Pinse is an archaic/literary past form (passato remoto) of a verb meaning roughly 'reached/touched' in this context; infino a = 'as far as', with l’occhio ('the eye' or 'gaze') as the thing that is reached.
segments.paradiso-20-g114: Tutto suo amor is the direct object 'all her love' (tutto + possessive suo). Là giù 'there below' is an adverbial phrase; pose is the passato remoto of porre ('put, placed') with the (omitted) subject L'altra. A drittura is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'on the right/straight path' or 'in rectitude'.
segments.paradiso-20-g115: Per che is a connective phrase meaning 'therefore' or 'for which reason' and introduces the result or consequence of the preceding clause.
segments.paradiso-20-g116: Di grazia in grazia is an adverbial idiom 'from grace to grace' indicating gradual progression or successive favors; the repetition with in marks movement or succession between states of grace.
segments.paradiso-20-g117: Dio is the subject; aperse is the passato remoto of aprire ('opened'). Li is a clitic dative/indirect-object pronoun ('to him/them') placed before the verb in this word order. L’occhio a la nostra redenzion futura is the object phrase 'the eye to our future redemption' meaning metaphorically 'opened their eyes to the coming redemption.'
segments.paradiso-20-g118: Ond’ is a contraction of 'onde' meaning 'whence' or 'therefore/from which'; ei is the old form for 'he' (or 'it/they' depending on referent). Credette is the passato remoto of credere ('believed'), and in quella ('in that') refers back to the thing believed (the redemption just mentioned).
segments.paradiso-20-g119: E non sofferse is 'and did not endure/tolerate' with sofferse the passato remoto of soffrire used here as 'to bear/put up with'. Da indi means 'from then/thereafter' (indi = thence/then), il puzzo più uses più after a negation to mean 'no longer' or 'any longer', and del paganesmo is 'of paganism' (the moral/spiritual stink of paganism).
segments.paradiso-20-g120: Riprendiene is a single word combining the verb riprende (present 'he/she reproves' or 'rebukes') with the enclitic ne referring back to those matters/people; grammatically it's third-person singular with an enclitic pronoun. Le genti perverse is the direct object 'the perverse peoples' that he rebukes.
segments.paradiso-20-g138: E come introduces a simile ('And as'). The phrase a buon cantor buon citarista places the beneficiary (a buon cantor, 'to a good singer') before the agent (buon citarista, 'a good lyre-player'); fa + infinitive seguitar is a causative construction meaning 'causes/makes ... follow'. Lo guizzo de la corda is the object: lo (archaic article) + guizzo ('a sudden twitch/flash') of the string (de la corda).
segments.paradiso-20-g139: In che here functions as 'by which' (introducing the effect of the guizzo). Più di piacer is a comparative 'more of pleasure' (i.e. 'more charm'), with piacer used as a noun. Lo canto acquista is a simple present 'the song acquires/gains', stating the resulting increase in pleasure.
segments.paradiso-20-g140: Sì here is a connective particle meaning 'so' or 'thus', linking the simile to the main clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-20-g141: Mentre introduces a temporal subordinate ('while'). Ch’ is the shortened che ('that/when'), and e' is the archaic contracted pronoun for 'he' (egli) followed by parlò (passato remoto 'he spoke'); the literal structure is 'while that he spoke' = 'while he spoke'. Note: the token listed as 'e' in the word list has senses for 'and' and does not match the pronoun function here, so no matching sense is available.
segments.paradiso-20-g142: Sì mi ricorda: sì ('thus') + ricorda ('reminds/recalls') with mi as the indirect object 'to me' — 'thus it recalls to me'. Ch’io vidi is an enclosed clause with vidi in the passato remoto ('I saw'), and le due luci benedette is the direct object 'the two blessed lights.'
segments.paradiso-20-g143: Pur come = 'even as' (pur adds emphasis). Batter d’occhi is a nominal phrase 'the beating/blinking of eyes' (batter used as a noun), and si concorda uses the clitic si to form a passive/reciprocal sense 'are in agreement/keep time with one another' — literally 'the beating of the eyes harmonizes (among themselves)'.
segments.paradiso-20-g144: Con le parole specifies the means ('with/by the words'). Mover is the archaic infinitive of muovere 'to move' (here used in an infinitival clause indicating what happens), and le fiammette is the plural diminutive 'the little flames' — the phrase says that the little flames (were) moved by the words.
segments.paradiso-20-g1: Quando begins a temporal subordinate clause ('when'). Colui (demonstrative/subject) + che introduces a relative clause 'who/that'; tutto 'the whole' modifies mondo. 'l (il) mondo alluma' is a poetic present-tense statement 'lights' (alluma = illumines/ kindles). 'de l’emisperio nostro' = 'from our hemisphere' (de + article). 'sì discende' = 'so descends' (sì = 'so', discende = present indicative 'descends'); the clause sets the time/condition for the main clause.
segments.paradiso-20-g2: che introduces the result/consequence clause 'that'. 'l giorno d’ogne parte' = 'the day of every part/place' (d’ = di). 'si consuma' is a reflexive/passive impersonally framed present 'is consumed/is spent' (si + verb = it becomes consumed).
segments.paradiso-20-g3: A brief nominative phrase: lo (definite article) + ciel (here 'the sky'/'heaven') is the subject of the main clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-20-g4: che introduces a restrictive relative clause modifying 'ciel'. Sol here is an archaic/poetic word (used as 'solo' = 'only') and di lui = 'by him'; prima s’accende is reflexive 'is first kindled' (s’ = si) — s’accende is present indicative in a poetic narrative sense, 'is first kindled by him'.
segments.paradiso-20-g5: subitamente 'suddenly'; si rifà uses the reflexive si with rifare in present to mean 'is remade/renewed'; parvente ('apparente' in sense of 'appearing') is a poetic adjective/adverb 'as if appearing'. per molte luci = 'by/through many lights' (per + plural noun).
segments.paradiso-20-g6: in che = 'in which' (relative clause); una refers back to 'luci' ('one [of them]') and risplende is present indicative 'shines brightly'. The clause specifies that among the many lights one particularly shines.
segments.paradiso-20-g7: e 'and'; questo atto del ciel is the subject ('this act of the sky'). mi venne a mente is a common idiom 'came to my mind' — venne is passato remoto (narrative past) 'came', mi = 'to me', a mente = 'into (my) mind'.
segments.paradiso-20-g8: come = 'as'/'just as' introducing a comparison; '’l segno del mondo e de’ suoi duci' = 'the sign of the world and of its leaders' (de’ = di + i), nel benedetto rostro = 'in the blessed beak/prow' (metaphorical location). fu tacente = passato remoto of essere + adjective tacente 'was silent' (tacente = 'silent').
segments.paradiso-20-g9: però che = 'because' (introducing a causal clause). tutte quelle vive luci = 'all those living/lively lights' (tutte = all; quelle = those; vive functioning as adjective 'living/vivid').
segments.paradiso-20-g10: vie più lucendo: vie (from via) with più forms an adverbial phrase 'ever more' / 'increasingly'; lucendo is a gerund from lucere 'shining' — 'shining more and more'.
segments.paradiso-20-g11: cominciaron is passato remoto 3rd person plural 'they began'. canti is the direct object 'songs' (or 'chants'); da mia memoria means 'from my memory' (here: 'in my memory'); labili e caduci are coordinate adjectives 'frail/unstable and perishable/transient'.
segments.paradiso-20-g12: O is the vocative interjection 'O/oh'. dolce amor is the addressed noun ('sweet Love'). che introduces a relative clause describing Love. di riso = 'with/of laughter'; t’ = clitic for 'ti' (you) as direct or reflexive object. ammanti is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'cloak/ mantle' or 'are mantled' (here used as a verb-like form referring back to the addressee), so the clause means 'who mantlest thyself in laughter.'
segments.paradiso-20-g13: quanto = exclamatory 'how'. parevi is imperfect indicative 2nd person singular 'you seemed/appeared' (parevi = parevi tu). ardente = 'ardent' / 'burning' (an adjective). in que' flailli = 'in those flails' (que' = quei, flailli = small flails / scourges; a poetic image).
segments.paradiso-20-g14: ch’ = che 'which/that'; avieno is imperfect (they had/possessed) in an older spelling (ave- form). spirto = 'spirit' (old spelling of spirito). sol = 'only' (archaic solo). di pensier santi = 'of holy thoughts'. The clause specifies that those objects had solely a spirit of holy thoughts.
segments.paradiso-20-g15: Poscia che = 'after' (temporal conjunction). i cari e lucidi lapilli is the subject phrase 'the dear and shining little stones.' ond’ = 'whence/from which' introduces the relative clause 'io vidi ingemmato il sesto lume' ('I saw the sixth light set with gems' — ingemmato past participle 'set with gems' modifying 'lume'). puoser (poetic passato remoto) is third-person plural 'they placed' / 'they caused' and silenzio a li angelici squilli = 'silence upon the angelic peals' (a + article + noun = upon/to the).
segments.paradiso-20-g16: udir mi parve = 'it seemed to me I heard' (udir infinitive + mi + parve 'seemed'). un mormorar di fiume = 'a murmur of a river' (mormorar is a verbal noun 'murmuring'). che scende chiaro giù di pietra in pietra = relative clause 'that descends clearly, down from stone to stone'; scende is present infinitive/indicative 'descends', chiaro = 'clear/bright', giù = 'down', di pietra in pietra = 'from rock to rock/stone by stone'.
segments.paradiso-20-g17: mostrando is a gerund 'showing' that links to the preceding clause; l’ubertà = 'the abundance/fertility' (ubertà = richness/abundance). del suo cacume = 'of its summit/crest' (suo = its; cacume = top/summit). The gerund shows the river's descent displaying the abundance of its crest.
segments.paradiso-20-g18: E = 'and'. come introduces a simile 'as/just as'. 'suono al collo de la cetra' = 'a sound on the neck of the lyre' (al = a + il). prende sua forma = prende (present indicative) 'takes' + sua forma 'its form/shape' — the sound acquires shape on the instrument's neck.
segments.paradiso-20-g19: e sì com’ = 'and just as'; al pertugio de la sampogna = 'to/at the hole of the bagpipe' (pertugio = small hole/opening; sampogna = bagpipe). vento che penètra = 'wind which penetrates' (penètra = present indicative 'penetrates' or 'enters'). The clause gives another similitude: the way wind enters a bagpipe hole.
segments.paradiso-20-g20: così = 'thus/so', marking the conclusion of the similes and linking to the main action that follows.
segments.paradiso-20-g21: rimosso d’aspettare indugio is an absolute/participial phrase: rimosso (past participle 'removed' or 'having dismissed'), d’aspettare = 'of waiting' (di + infinitive), indugio = 'delay/hesitation'. Together: 'having removed any delay in waiting' or idiomatically 'without further delay'.
segments.paradiso-20-g22: quel mormorar = 'that murmur' (demonstrative + noun). de l’aguglia = 'of the aguglia' (aguglia can be an eagle/needle/spire — a noun in the genitive). salissi is an archaic/poetic verbal form (here used as an aoristic/poetic past—third-person singular) meaning 'rose/leapt up' (cf. salire). su per lo collo = 'up along the neck' (su per = up along).
segments.paradiso-20-g23: come = 'as/like'; fosse is the imperfect subjunctive of essere 'were' used in a simile ('as if it were'); bugio is an archaic noun meaning 'a monkey' (used here as a comparative image).
segments.paradiso-20-g44: E quel che segue = 'And he who follows' (quel + relative che + segu(e)). Word order is literary: quel (that one) + che (relative 'who/which') + segue (present 'follows' / 'comes next'). in la circunferenza 'in la' (archaic separation instead of modern nella) is a locative phrase 'in the circumference/surrounding ring'; di che ragiono is a relative-of reference 'of which I speak' where che is a relative pronoun and ragiono is 1st person present 'I speak/argue'.
segments.paradiso-20-g45: per l’arco superno = 'by/through the heavenly arc.' Here per means 'by means of/through' and l’ is the elided definite article; arco is 'arch/arc' and superno is an elevated/poetic adjective 'heavenly/upper'.
segments.paradiso-20-g46: morte indugiò per vera penitenza: — literal order puts 'morte' before the verb: 'death tarried/was delayed.' Indugiò is passato remoto (third person) of indugiare 'to delay, tarry.' 'Per vera penitenza' expresses the means or cause: 'by/through true penance.'
segments.paradiso-20-g47: ora conosce che ’l giudicio etterno non si trasmuta = 'now he knows that the eternal judgment does not change.' conosce is present indicative (he knows/realizes); the clause after che is a declarative subordinate. si here is the reflexive/passive particle used to form a passive/impersonal sense ('is changed' rather than a literal reflexive). trasmuta in context means 'is altered/changes' (an archaic or poetic verb form/spelling).
segments.paradiso-20-g48: quando degno preco fa crastino là giù de l’odïerno = 'when a worthy supplication makes tomorrow down there out of today.' quando introduces a temporal clause; degno modifies preco ('a worthy / fitting plea'); preco is an abstract noun 'entreaty/plea/prayer'; fa is causative 'makes' or 'causes'; crastino is a noun 'morrow/tomorrow'; là giù 'down there' (i.e. on earth); de l’odïerno literally 'of the present day' — the phrase means the plea causes what is 'today' below to be put off to 'tomorrow.'
segments.paradiso-20-g49: L’altro che segue = 'The other who follows.' L’ (elided article) + altro (the other/another) + che (relative 'who/which') + segue (present 'follows'/'comes next'). It identifies the next person in sequence.
segments.paradiso-20-g50: con le leggi e meco = 'with the laws and with me.' con is the simple preposition 'with'; le leggi is plural 'the laws' (can mean rules or scriptures in context); e meco is 'and with me' — meco is an archaic prepositional pronoun meaning 'with me.'
segments.paradiso-20-g51: sotto buona intenzion che fé mal frutto = 'under a good intention that produced ill fruit.' sotto here means 'under the guise/cover of'; buona intenzion is 'good intention'; che introduces a relative clause; fé is passato remoto of fare 'it made/produced'; mal frutto = 'bad fruit' (i.e. a bad result).
segments.paradiso-20-g52: per cedere al pastor si fece greco: = 'in order to yield/submit to the shepherd he made himself Greek.' per + infinitive indicates purpose ('in order to'); cedere al pastor = 'to yield to the shepherd' (pastor = spiritual shepherd/pastor); si fece is reflexive/periphrastic past of fare 'he made himself / became'; greco = 'Greek' (here likely meaning 'adopted Greek ways' or 'appeared Greek' — a figurative disguise).
segments.paradiso-20-g53: ora conosce come il mal dedutto dal suo bene operar non li è nocivo = 'now he knows how the evil derived from his good action is not harmful to him.' conosce (he knows) + subordinate introduced by come ('how/that'); il mal dedutto dal suo bene operar is a nominal phrase 'the harm derived from his good action(s)'; non li è nocivo uses li (here an indirect-object pronoun 'to him') before the verb è 'is' and nocivo 'harmful.'
segments.paradiso-20-g54: avvegna che sia ’l mondo indi distrutto = 'although the world be thereby destroyed.' avvegna che is a concessive conjunction meaning 'although/though' and is followed by the subjunctive sia; l’ mondo = 'the world'; indi distrutto = 'thence/thereby destroyed' (indi = 'from that, thereby/thereupon').
segments.paradiso-20-g55: E quel che vedi ne l’arco declivo = 'And that which you see in the sloping/descending arch.' ne = 'in it/in the' (ne + article), l’arco declivo is a descriptive locative 'the sloping arch'; che is the relative 'which' and vedi is second-person present 'you see.'
segments.paradiso-20-g56: Guiglielmo fu = 'William was.' Fu is passato remoto (third person singular) of essere and gives the historical/epic past 'he was.' The phrase is a simple identificatory clause naming the person.
segments.paradiso-20-g57: cui quella terra plora che piagne Carlo e Federigo vivo: — cui is a dative/relative 'for whom' or 'to whom'; quella terra plora 'that land laments/mourns' (plora = weeps); the following che introduces a relative clause 'which Charles and Frederick lament while alive' where piagne is present indicative 'they lament' and vivo postpositively qualifies (i.e. 'while alive'). The syntax links the mourning of the land to the named living princes.
segments.paradiso-20-g58: ora conosce come s’innamora lo ciel del giusto rege = 'now he knows how heaven falls in love with the just king.' conosce (he knows) + subordinate introduced by come ('how'); s’innamora is the pronominal verb innamorarsi (reflexive), here meaning 'is enamored' or 'loves'; lo ciel (poetic order) is 'the heaven' and del giusto rege = 'of the just king.'
segments.paradiso-20-g59: e al sembiante del suo fulgore il fa vedere ancora = 'and to the semblance/appearance of his brightness he makes him be seen again.' al + sembiante = 'to the appearance/face'; del suo fulgore = 'of his radiance/splendor'; il is the direct object pronoun ('him' referring back to William) placed before fa (causative fare) + vedere ('see'), so fa vedere = 'causes (him) to be seen' and ancora = 'again/still.'
segments.paradiso-20-g60: Chi crederebbe giù nel mondo errante che Rifëo Troiano in questo tondo fosse la quinta de le luci sante? = a rhetorical question. Chi crederebbe is conditional 'who would believe'; giù nel mondo errante = 'down in the wandering world' (i.e. on earth); che introduces the subordinate clause; fosse is imperfect subjunctive (or past subjunctive) of essere used because the main verb is conditional; la quinta de le luci sante = 'the fifth of the holy lights' (i.e. the fifth of the blessed/holy spheres/lamps).
segments.paradiso-20-g121: Quelle tre donne — a straightforward noun phrase with demonstrative + cardinal number + noun: 'Quelle' is a demonstrative 'those'; 'tre donne' = 'three women'. 'li fur per battesmo' uses the archaic past-remote plural 'fur' (from essere: 'they were') and the preposition 'per' with 'battesmo' meaning 'by/through baptism'. The clitic 'li' here is archaic/dialectal (dative/benefactive 'to him' or similar) rather than the modern article; it sits before the verb as older Italian enclitic clitics often do. The relative 'che' introduces the clause 'che tu vedesti da la destra rota' ('whom you saw on the right of the wheel').
segments.paradiso-20-g122: 'dinanzi al battezzar' is a temporal/prepositional phrase: 'dinanzi' = 'before', followed by the contracted article+preposition 'al' + the infinitive noun-like verb 'battezzar' (an infinitive used substantively = 'the baptizing' or 'the baptism'). 'più d’un millesmo' is a comparative quantifier: 'più' = 'more', 'd’' = 'di' used in the comparative sense 'than', 'un millesmo' = 'a thousandth' (here a measure of amount/time/degree).
segments.paradiso-20-g123: A short apostrophe: 'O' is the vocative interjection 'oh', addressing 'predestinazion' (predestination) directly; this is a rhetorical opening that sets up an exclamatory question in the next segment.
segments.paradiso-20-g124: This is an exclamatory clause. 'quanto' = 'how' (introducing the exclamation); 'remota' is an adjective meaning 'remote/distant' modifying 'la radice tua' ('your root') and sits before the copula 'è' ('is'). 'da quelli aspetti' = 'from those aspects' (preposition 'da' + plural demonstrative 'quelli' + noun 'aspetti'). The relative clause 'che la prima cagion non veggion tota' qualifies 'quelli aspetti': 'che' = 'which', 'la prima cagion' = 'the First Cause' (God), 'non veggion' is an archaic/pronominal form meaning 'do not see' (here an older form of vedere), and 'tota' = 'wholly/entire'.
segments.paradiso-20-g125: 'E voi' = 'And you' — simple coordinating conjunction + personal pronoun; this begins an address to the hearers/readers.
segments.paradiso-20-g126: A vocative/parenthetic noun 'mortali' (mortal men/people) placed after the address for emphasis; it is nominative plural and directly addresses human listeners.
segments.paradiso-20-g127: 'tenetevi stretti a giudicar' is an imperative/reflexive construction: 'tenetevi' (imperative plural of tenere + reflexive) means 'hold yourselves' or 'keep yourselves' (here 'hold back' or 'be restrained'); 'stretti' is the past-participle/adjectival complement 'tight/close' describing the state to keep; 'a giudicar' is the infinitive 'to judge' introduced by 'a' (purpose/limit). Together: 'beware of judging' / 'keep yourselves tight from judging.'
segments.paradiso-20-g128: 'ché noi,' begins the explanatory clause: 'ché' = 'for/because', and 'noi' = 'we' (subject of the following relative clause).
segments.paradiso-20-g129: Relative clause 'che Dio vedemo' modifies 'noi': the relative 'che' = 'who/which', 'Dio' = 'God', and 'vedemo' is the present indicative 'we see/behold' (archaic form; modern Italian 'vediamo'). It qualifies the subject 'we' as 'we who see God'.
segments.paradiso-20-g130: 'non conosciamo ancor tutti li eletti' is a declarative clause: 'non conosciamo' = 'we do not know/recognize', 'ancor' = 'yet/still', 'tutti' = 'all', 'li eletti' = 'the elect' (definite article 'li' + past participle used as noun 'eletti'). The clause states that even those who see God do not yet know all the chosen ones.
segments.paradiso-20-g131: 'ed ènne dolce così fatto scemo' is compact/poetic: 'ed' = 'and'; 'ènne' is an archaic/clitic form (contextually something like 'from that/thereof' or 'to it') and is irregular/archaic in form; 'dolce' = 'sweet/pleasant'; 'così fatto scemo' = 'made so foolish' ('fatto' = past participle 'made', 'scemo' = 'foolish/ignorant'). The whole phrase means 'and from that it is pleasant to be made so foolish' — i.e., there is sweetness/pleasure in being made ignorant in that way.
segments.paradiso-20-g132: 'perché il ben nostro in questo ben s’affina' explains the reason: 'perché' = 'because', 'il ben nostro' = 'our good' (literally 'the good of ours'), 'in questo ben' = 'in this good' (locative/instrumental), and 's’affina' is reflexive 'si affina' (present indicative) meaning 'is/gets refined' or 'becomes purified'. Grammatically this is a causal clause introduced by 'perché'.
segments.paradiso-20-g133: Result/summary clause: 'che quel che vole Iddio' — the first 'che' functions as a resumptive conjunction 'so that/that', 'quel' = 'that which', the second 'che' = relative 'which', and 'vole Iddio' = 'God wills/wants'. Together: 'so that what God wills...'.
segments.paradiso-20-g134: Clos ing clause 'e noi volemo' = 'and we will (it)' — 'e' = 'and', 'noi' = 'we', 'volemo' is an older present tense form meaning 'we will/wish' (modern 'vogliamo' or 'vogliamo volere'); it completes the sense: 'what God wills, we will as well.'
segments.paradiso-20-g135: 'Così da quella imagine divina' is a prepositional phrase: 'Così' = 'thus/so', 'da' = 'from/by', 'quella imagine divina' = 'that divine image' (demonstrative + noun + adjective). It sets the source/agentual image from which what follows proceeds.
segments.paradiso-20-g136: 'per farmi chiara la mia corta vista' is a purpose clause introduced by 'per' ('in order to'): 'farmi' = 'to make me' (infinitive + clitic), 'chiara' = 'clear' (adjective agreeing with 'vista'), 'la mia corta vista' = 'my short/limited sight' (direct object). The infinitive phrase expresses the intended effect on the poet's perception.
segments.paradiso-20-g137: 'data mi fu soave medicina' uses an inverted order with the compound verb 'fu data' (past passive): 'data' (past participle 'given') comes first for emphasis, 'mi' = 'to me', 'fu' = 'was' (past perfect/passive auxiliary), 'soave medicina' = 'gentle/sweet remedy' (adjective + noun). The whole is a passive construction meaning 'a gentle remedy was given to me.'

sentences.paradiso-20-s5: A voice was made there, and then from his beak it issued in the form of words, the very ones the heart had been awaiting where I wrote them.
sentences.paradiso-20-s6: "The part in me that sees and seems to feel the sun in the mortal eagles," they began to me, "now wishes to look fixedly, because of the fires from which I was formed in figure, those from which the eye in my head sparkles; and of all their degrees these are the summits."
sentences.paradiso-20-s7: He who shines in the middle through the pupil was the singer of the Holy Spirit, who moved the ark from village to village; now he knows the merit of his song, insofar as it was the result of his counsel, because of the recompense which is equivalent.
sentences.paradiso-20-s8: Of the five who make a ring around me along my eyelid, the one who comes closest to my beak comforted the little widow about her son; now he knows how dearly it costs not to follow Christ, from the experience of this sweet life and of the opposite.
sentences.paradiso-20-s17: Then, with his gaze more kindled, the blessed sign answered me so as not to leave me suspended in wonder: "I see that you believe these things because I say them, but you do not see how; so that, if they are believed, they are hidden."
sentences.paradiso-20-s18: Do as those who learn a thing well by name do, but they cannot see its essence unless someone else presents it.
sentences.paradiso-20-s19: The kingdom of heaven suffers a violence from ardent love and from living hope, which overcomes the divine will: not in the way that one man dominates another, but it conquers her because she wishes to be conquered, and, conquered, conquers by her own benignity.
sentences.paradiso-20-s20: The first movement of the eyelid and the fifth astonish you, because in them you see the realm of the angels depicted.
sentences.paradiso-20-s13: Now it knows much of that which the world cannot see of divine grace, although its sight does not discern the depth.
sentences.paradiso-20-s14: As a little lark that ranges in the air, first singing and then silent, content with the final sweetness that satisfies it, so appeared to me the image of the imprint of eternal pleasure, at whose desire every thing becomes what it is.
sentences.paradiso-20-s15: And although I was there, in my doubting, almost like glass to the colour that clothes it, I could not endure waiting in silence for a while; instead from my mouth came, "What are these things?"
sentences.paradiso-20-s16: , it pressed me with the force of its weight: so that I, by the flashing, saw great feasts.
sentences.paradiso-20-s21: They did not come out of their bodies, as you think, o Gentiles, but were Christians; steadfast in faith were the one of the passuri and the one of the passi piedi.
sentences.paradiso-20-s22: For one of them from Hell — where one never returns of his own free will — returned already to the bones; and this was a reward of living hope: of living hope that put power into the prayers made to God to raise her, so that her will might be moved.
sentences.paradiso-20-s23: The glorious soul I have mentioned, having returned to the flesh in which she had been but briefly, believed in Him who could help her; and, believing, she was kindled into so great a fire of true love that at the second death she was worthy to come to this game.
sentences.paradiso-20-s24: The other, by a grace that drips from so deep a fountain that no creature ever reached its gaze as far as the first wave, laid all her love down there on the straight path: therefore, from grace to grace God opened to them the eye to our future redemption; whence he believed in it, and from then on no longer endured the stench of paganism; and reproves the perverse peoples.
sentences.paradiso-20-s29: And as a good lyre-player makes a good singer follow the quick twitch of the string — by which the song gains more charm — so, while he spoke, it thus reminded me that I had seen the two blessed lights; even as the beating of the eyes kept time with one another, the little flames moved with the words.
sentences.paradiso-20-s1: When He who lights the whole world descends from our hemisphere so that the day in every region is consumed, the sky — which alone is first kindled by him — is suddenly remade, appearing through many lights in which one shines; and this act of the sky came into my mind, as the sign of the world and of its leaders in the blessed prow was silent; because all those living lights, shining ever more, began songs that my memory found frail and transient.
sentences.paradiso-20-s2: O sweet Love, who dost mantle thyself in laughter, how ardent you seemed in those little flails, which had only the spirit of holy thoughts!
sentences.paradiso-20-s3: After those dear and shining little stones — with which I saw the sixth light set with gems — had laid silence upon the angelic peals, it seemed to me I heard a murmur of a river that descends clearly, down from stone to stone, showing the abundance of its summit.
sentences.paradiso-20-s4: And as a sound on the neck of the lyre takes its form, and just as wind that penetrates the hole of the bagpipe, so, dismissing any delay in waiting, that murmur of the aguglia rose up along the neck, as if it were a monkey.
sentences.paradiso-20-s9: And he who follows in the surrounding ring I am speaking of, by the heavenly arc, postponed death through true penance; now he knows that the eternal judgment is not changed when a worthy plea makes tomorrow down there out of today.
sentences.paradiso-20-s10: The other who follows, with the laws and with me, under a good intention that bore bad fruit, in order to yield to the shepherd made himself Greek; now he knows how the evil derived from his good action is not harmful to him, even though the world is thereby destroyed.
sentences.paradiso-20-s11: And he whom you see in the downward arc was William, for whom that land laments which Charles and Frederick lament while alive; now he knows how heaven falls in love with the just king, and shows him again in the likeness of his brightness.
sentences.paradiso-20-s12: Who would believe, down in the wandering world, that Rifeo the Trojan in this circle would be the fifth of the holy lights?
sentences.paradiso-20-s25: Those three women were his by baptism, whom you saw on the right of the wheel, before the baptism by more than a thousandth.
sentences.paradiso-20-s26: O predestination, how remote is your root from those aspects which the First Cause does not behold wholly!
sentences.paradiso-20-s27: And you, mortals, hold yourselves back from judging: for we, who behold God, do not yet know all the elect; and from that it is sweet to be made so foolish, because our good is refined in this good, so that what God wills, we will (too).
sentences.paradiso-20-s28: Thus, from that divine image, to make my limited sight clear, a gentle remedy was given to me.
